auth_notes: Requires two custom headers: 'x-app-id' and 'x-api-key'. Some endpoints for user-specific actions may require a JWT Bearer token generated via the sign-in endpoint.
rate_limits: Typically 60 requests per minute per project, though this varies based on the specific license and server configuration.
gotchas: The API often returns a 200 OK status even if the response body contains an 'error': true flag, requiring manual error handling logic in n8n.
Data structures for orders are deeply nested, necessitating the use of the Item Lists node or Code node for flattening.
Webhook events must be manually configured in the Ordering dashboard to point to the n8n production URL.
research_notes: Since no native node exists, the HTTP Request node is mandatory. You will need to set up a 'Header Auth' credential type in n8n to store the x-api-key and x-app-id. For webhooks, use the n8n Webhook node and register the URL in the Ordering.co 'Developers' section under Webhooks.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Since no native node exists, the HTTP Request node is mandatory. You will need to set up a 'Header Auth' credential type in n8n to store the x-api-key and x-app-id. For webhooks, use the n8n Webhook node and register the URL in the Ordering.co 'Developers' section under Webhooks.
